hypothesis: Multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization that uses polymer-specific language model embeddings (particularly PolyNC) as the molecular representation for the surrogate model will achieve superior sample efficiency when correlating fast MD/GCMC estimates with long-timescale MD validation data, because the structured chemical token embeddings of polymer-specific models will better capture the backbone connectivity features that govern the systematic discrepancy between low-fidelity and high-fidelity CO2 uptake estimates, enabling the multi-fidelity surrogate to learn a more accurate inter-fidelity correlation function and thereby require fewer expensive high-fidelity simulations to identify top-performing branched polyamine sorbents compared to multi-fidelity frameworks using general chemical space representations.
